content: Botticelli, whose full name is Alessandro di Mariano di Vanni Filipepi, was an Italian painter of the Early Renaissance. He is also known as Sandro Botticelli or simply Botticelli.

Botticelli lived all his life in the same neighbourhood of Florence. Specifically, he lived in the Ognissanti neighbourhood. There were a couple of exceptions, where he spent some time painting elsewhere: Pisa in 1474 and Rome (specifically, the Sistine Chapel) from 1481-82.
What were his most famous paintings?
Botticelli's best-known works are "The Birth of Venus" and "Primavera", both located in the Uffizi in Florence.

Various payments up to September 1474 were recorded, indicating that Botticelli was financially supported by the authorities in Pisa. Additionally, his family rented a house from the wealthy Rucellai family, who also had dealings with Botticelli's family in other ways.
William Young Ottley was an English collector who bought Botticelli's The Mystical Nativity in Italy and brought it to London in 1799.
What did he do with the painting?

When Ottley tried to sell the painting in 1811, no buyer could be found. After his death, it was purchased by William Fuller Maitland of Stansted, who allowed it to be exhibited at a major art exhibition in Manchester in 1857, where it was viewed by over a million people.
